mission.title: virtual-kubelet: What to do with providers?
mission.description: What to do with providers?. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current virtual-kubelet deployment
steps.description:
Verify your virtual-kubelet version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n virtual-kubelet -l app.kubernetes.io/name=virtual-kubelet
helm list -n virtual-kubelet 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working virtual-kubelet installation.

steps.title: Review virtual-kubelet configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant virtual-kubelet configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n virtual-kubelet -l app.kubernetes.io/name=virtual-kubelet
kubectl get configmap -n virtual-kubelet -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=virtual-kubelet
```
Right now this repo is full of provider specific code which also has a separate maintenance flow from VK core.

steps.title: Apply the fix for What to do with providers?
steps.description:
Add OpenStack Zun provider.

Zun primarily uses the concepts of Container and Capsule. A container is a single container backed by Docker or other container engines. The basic Docker primitives, such as create/delete container, are integrated with Zun. In addition, Zun adds several OpenStack-integrated capabilities to containers. For example, a Zun container is, by default, given Neutron IP addresses, has an option to attach Cinder volumes, the access is authenticated by Keystone and several

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n virtual-kubelet -l app.kubernetes.io/name=virtual-kubelet
kubectl get events -n virtual-kubelet --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "What to do with providers?" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Add OpenStack Zun provider.

Zun primarily uses the concepts of Container and Capsule. A container is a single container backed by Docker or other container engines. The basic Docker primitives, such as create/delete container, are integrated with Zun. In addition, Zun adds several OpenStack-integrated capabilities to containers.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with virtual-kubelet installed or the issue environment reproducible.
